title: Other skilled trades n.e.c.
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Design, cut, and arrange live, dried, or artificial flowers and foliage. Design, measure, fit, and adapt orthopedic braces, appliances or prostheses, such as limbs or facial parts for patients with disabling conditions. (Based on 83 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information

Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General

Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Read prescriptions or specifications to determine the type of product or device to be fabricated and the materials and tools required.
Instruct patients in the use and care of orthotic or prosthetic devices and appliances.
Take patients' body or limb measurements and make plaster casts for use in device construction.
Construct or receive casts or impressions of patients' limbs for use as patterns for fabrication.
Bend, form, and shape materials to conform to prescribed contours of components for prosthetic or orthotic devices.
Drill, glue, weld, bolt, or rivet parts together to form prosthetic or orthotic devices.
Layout, mark dimensions, and cover or pad structural components using various materials like rubber or leather.
Polish and repair artificial limbs, braces, or supports, using grinding and buffing tools.
Fabricate, alter, or repair dental devices such as dentures, crowns, or orthodontic appliances.
Fit, test, and evaluate devices on patients; make adjustments to ensure proper fit and function.
Design orthopedic and prosthetic devices based on assessments and specifications from physicians.
Train and supervise other technicians and support staff involved in the construction of orthopedic or prosthetic devices.
Research new methods for constructing and using orthopedic and prosthetic devices, attending relevant conferences to update knowledge.
Maintain patients' records and consult with physicians regarding specifications for orthopedic or prosthetic devices.
Serve customers and provide information about the care and maintenance of floral arrangements and related items.
Research catalogs or repair part lists to locate sources for repair parts and record their receipt.
String instruments and adjust trusses and bridges to obtain specified string tensions and heights.
Repair or replace musical instrument parts using hand and power tools.
Polish instruments using rags, compounds, or burnishing tools.
Mix and measure glue for instrument repair.
Reassemble instruments after repair using necessary tools and lubricate instruments as required.
Inspect instruments to locate defects and determine the level of restoration required.
Disassemble instruments and parts for repair and adjustment.
Test and calibrate components or equipment following manufacturers' manuals and troubleshooting techniques.
Clean parts using cleaning solutions, air hoses, and cloths.
Confer with customers to determine desired material and garment styles.
Fit, alter, repair, and make clothing according to customers' specifications and principles of garment design.
Perform preventive maintenance or service on equipment, such as cleaning and lubricating.
Read and interpret engineering drawings and specifications to determine needed repairs or assembly instructions.
Adjust and repair electrical or electronic components to correct defects and ensure conformity to specifications.
Measure and visually inspect products for surface and dimension defects to ensure conformance to specifications.
Attach workpieces to grinding machines, form specified sections, and repair cracks using welding or brazing equipment.
Dip workpieces in electroplating solutions or apply pigments to highlight surfaces.
Straighten workpieces, remove dents, and inspect them to ensure specifications are met using various tools.
Study blueprints or layouts to determine grinding procedures, plan setups, and operational sequences.
Unload finished products, pack them in containers, and place containers in warehouses.
Pour or load metal or sand into casting equipment like molds or hoppers using various tools.
File or finish surfaces of workpieces and assemble parts or units according to specifications.
Select and mount grinding wheels on machines and perform basic maintenance on machinery.
Measure components to determine tolerances and adjust tools for accurate cuts.
Inspect components of timepieces for defects using precision measuring instruments or loupes.
Disassemble and repair timepieces, ensuring operation and fit of parts.
Examine and test electrical components for defects and record results using measuring devices.
Spread, level, or smooth concrete or terrazzo mixtures to form bases using various tools.
Chip, scrape, and grind high spots in concrete or terrazzo using pneumatic or hand tools.
Mix cement, sand, and water to produce concrete or grout, and prepare surfaces for bonding.
Create and modify jewelry designs, inspect finished products, and ensure they conform to specifications.
Select and acquire materials for jewelry designs and produce them by cutting, shaping, and polishing stones.
Advise customers on the best use of gems for jewelry and estimate their market value based on quality and pricing.
Select shaping wheels for tasks, and mix and apply abrasives or polishing compounds.
Use different sewing techniques such as felling, tacking, and embroidery.
Sew buttonholes or add lace and trimming to items.
Tie, knit, weave, or knot ribbon or decorative materials.
Examine and ensure accuracy of gems during processing using magnifying tools.
Hold stones or gems against tools to cut, shape, or grind them.
Examine, measure, or test sample products to ensure they meet specifications.
Produce or assemble components of articles like furniture or fixtures.
Repair or alter wooden furniture and cabinetry.
Install hardware such as hinges and handles using hand tools.
Take measurements and mark dimensions for cutting wood based on blueprints.
Trim, sand, and shape surfaces using appropriate hand tools.
Clean and lubricate cutting machines and tools.
Spray surfaces with primers and decorative or finish coatings.
Design and layout paver installation patterns and specifications.
Discuss furniture design with clients to determine styles and finishes.
Monitor operations to identify flaws in finishes and correct them.
Select and mix ingredients to prepare coating substances according to specifications, using paddles or mechanical mixers.
Clean and maintain tools and equipment, using solvents, brushes, and rags.
Rinse, drain, or wipe coated workpieces to remove excess coating material or to facilitate setting of finish coats on workpieces.
Unload mixtures into containers or onto conveyors for further processing.
Examine materials, ingredients, or products visually or with hands to ensure conformance to established standards.
Dislodge and clear jammed materials or other items from machinery or equipment, using hand tools.
Hire, train, or supervise new employees or apprentices.
Apply coatings, such as paint, ink, or lacquer, to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces, using spray guns, pens, or brushes.
Compound or process ingredients or dyes according to formulas.
Clean surfaces of workpieces in preparation for coating, using cleaning fluids, solvents, brushes, or cloth.
Read work orders to determine production specifications or information.
Examine finished surfaces of workpieces to verify conformance to specifications and retouch any defective areas.
Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps or conveyors, to move materials or ingredients through production processes.
Transfer materials, supplies, or products between work areas, using moving equipment or hand tools.
Record operational or production data on specified forms.
Read job orders and inspect workpieces to determine work procedures and materials required.
Operate sandblasting equipment to roughen and clean surfaces of workpieces.
Mix or blend ingredients by starting machines and mixing for specified times.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing.
Conduct pressure tests on vessels, such as boilers.
Clean work areas.
Fabricate or alter parts at construction sites using tools like shears, hammers, punches, or drills.
Tend machines to mix or blend a variety of materials such as chemicals or food products.
Repair or replace defective pressure vessel parts using various tools and techniques.
Inspect coated or plated areas for defects such as bubbles or uneven coverage.
Remove excess materials or impurities from objects using grinding machines or air hoses.
Monitor inventory levels on a regular basis, ordering or requesting additional supplies as necessary.
Mark defects such as cracks and splits for repair.
Collect and inspect samples to identify necessary adjustments and assess quality compliance for production processes.
Clean and maintain equipment, including performing routine inspections, making minor repairs, and ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Measure, weigh, and process materials or ingredients at specified intervals to ensure conformance to requirements.
Cut, shape, and form materials using machines and hand tools to prescribed dimensions and finishes.
Inspect and test products or components for defects or excessive wear, ensuring compliance with specifications.
Assemble mechanical and electrical components into products using hand tools and power tools according to specifications.
Repair or replace malfunctioning components of machinery or equipment, ensuring they are maintained in operational condition.
Record and maintain documentation regarding production, repairs, and machine maintenance information.
Generate new designs or modify existing designs for tools, dies, and machines, using computer-aided design technologies.
Install, maintain, and repair specialized machinery or systems, ensuring efficient operation and safety compliance.
Operate newly repaired machinery or equipment to verify the adequacy of repairs.
Disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts and make repairs, including using hand tools and cranes.
Inventory and requisition machine parts and supplies to maintain and replenish stock as necessary.
Transport machine parts, tools, and equipment between work areas and storage, using cranes, hoists, or dollies.
Clean machines and machine parts, using cleaning solvents, cloths, air guns, hoses, or vacuums.
Observe and test the operation of machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions using testing devices.
Inspect articles for defects, and remove damaged or worn parts using hand tools.
Repair and recondition leather products, including shoes, using various machines and materials.
Make, modify, and repair orthopedic or therapeutic footwear according to prescriptions for foot problems.
Prepare workpieces for etching or engraving by cutting, sanding, and treating them.
Inspect etched work for depth and defects using calibrated tools and gauges.
Troubleshoot or repair mechanical, hydraulic, or electrical malfunctioning systems in machinery.
Repair, fabricate, and install steel or wood fittings according to blueprints and specifications.
Weld, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery as per required standards.
Assemble machines and install components using hand tools and power tools according to design specs.
Examine equipment and confer with customers to locate and diagnose malfunctions effectively.
Perform routine maintenance, cleaning, and lubrication of equipment and vehicles.
Record details of repairs made and parts used during maintenance activities.
Clean and polish engraved areas to maintain quality and presentation of finished workpieces.
Collect and discard worn machine parts to maintain machinery and work areas.
Align machines or equipment, using hoists, jacks, hand tools, squares, rules, micrometers, lasers, or plumb bobs.
Cut lengths of tubing to specified sizes using files or cutting wheels.
Design and create glass objects using blowpipes and artisans' hand tools and equipment.
Patch broken edges or fractures using clay or plaster; repair broken scrolls by replacing them with new tubing sections.
Select sizes and types of molds according to instructions; assemble, insert, and adjust wires, tubes, cores, fittings, rods, or patterns into molds.
Operate and adjust controls of heating equipment to melt material or cure, dry, or bake filled molds.
Align and assemble parts to produce completed products using gauges and hand tools; inspect or measure finished workpieces for conformance to specifications.
Set the proper operating temperature for each casting; load filled molds in ovens or dryers.
Clean, finish, and lubricate molds and mold parts; clean work area.
Mix and apply glazes to pottery pieces, using tools such as spray guns; fire pottery pieces in gas or electric kilns.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale; design spaces to display pottery for sale.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand using a variety of methods and materials.
Sketch or draw objects to be crafted; develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects.
Select materials for use based on strength, color, texture, and other characteristics; set specifications for materials, dimensions, and finishes.
Confer with customers to assess customer needs or obtain feedback; research craft trends and customer buying patterns to inspire designs.
Pack products for shipping and apply finishes to objects being crafted.
Develop product packaging, display, and pricing strategies.
Remove worn or damaged plastic block refractory linings of furnaces, using hand tools.
Reline or repair ladles and pouring spouts with refractory clay, using trowels.
Chip slag from linings of ladles or remove linings when beyond repair, using hammers and chisels.
Measure furnace walls to determine dimensions and cut required number of sheets from plastic block, using saws.
Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, mortar powder, and water to form refractory clay or mortar, using shovels or mixing machines.
Climb scaffolding and spray surfaces of cupolas with refractory mixtures, using spray equipment.
Dump and tamp clay in molds, using tamping tools, and transfer clay structures to curing ovens.
Install equipment such as guides, guards, gears, cooling equipment, and rolls, using hand tools.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations, using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses, or welding equipment.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work, using devices such as hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards.
Monitor tool and part inventories and the condition and maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions.
